source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Lifeboat Manual Winch

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - High Island Block A-5
context.operator: Manta Ray Gathering Company, L.L.C / Genesis Energy LP

event.top_event: Head injury from winch assist handle strike
actions_taken: USCG transport to UTMB Galveston at 0957 hours
Injured person underwent skull fracture surgery
Genesis scheduled contract survival craft specialists for onsite training
Genesis developing JSHA for routine tasks including winch operations
Thorough review of operational procedures and checklists planned
event.summary: On July 11, 2024, at 0730 hours, a contract pipeline operator from Quality Process Services suffered a serious head injury when struck by a winch assist handle during a routine lifeboat capsule inspection at the High Island A-5 Manta Ray Gathering Facility. The handle remained in the socket when the powered winch was engaged, causing it to rotate and strike the operator in the head. The operator sustained a fractured skull requiring surgery and was transported via USCG to UTMB in Galveston. The incident was caused by failure to remove the winch assist handle before engaging the powered winch, compounded by a bypassed safety device and lack of a Job Safety Analysis.
recommendations: Conduct onsite training for all operators on proper winch operation and safety protocols
Develop and implement a Job Safety and Health Analysis (JSHA) for routine tasks including winch operations
Review and update operational procedures and checklists to ensure all necessary safety measures are included
Ensure winch assist handle removal is a mandatory step before powered winch engagement

hazards.name: Rotating winch mechanism with removable handle
Personnel proximity to moving equipment during winch operation

threats.name: Winch assist handle left in socket during powered winch operation
Safety device bypass allowing winch operation with handle in place
Operator positioned at upper davit section during winch operation

consequences.name: Head strike injury from rotating winch assist handle
Loss of personnel availability due to medical evacuation and treatment

controls.name: Winch assist handle removal before powered winch engagement
controls.barrier_role: Prevent handle from rotating and striking personnel
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to eliminate hazard source

supporting_text: The winch assist handle should have been removed before engaging the powered winch
The winch assist is a removable metal handle designed for manual cranking of the vessel to the stops and should only be inserted after the powered winch is disengaged and stored when not in use

controls.name: Safety device to prevent winch operation with handle in place
controls.barrier_role: Prevent powered winch engagement when handle is inserted
controls.lod_basis: Secondary engineering control to prevent unsafe operation

supporting_text: A safety to override the handle in place and power the winch was later found to be bypassed

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) or Job Safety and Health Analysis (JSHA) for winch operations
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and establish safe work procedures for routine tasks
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure hazard awareness and procedure compliance

supporting_text: The IP and the PIC opted to use a weekly inspection checklist for this task instead of preparing a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) together with the checklist
Lack of Job Safety Analysis (JSA): The task was considered routine, and no JSA or Job Safety and Health Analysis (JSHA) was conducted

controls.name: Weekly inspection checklist for capsule inspection
controls.barrier_role: Ensure systematic inspection of equipment and procedures
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control for routine task management

supporting_text: The weekly checklist did not include the actual operation of the winch

controls.name: Personnel positioning away from moving equipment during winch operation
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel exposure to rotating hazards
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to maintain safe distance from hazards

supporting_text: The IP was positioned on the upper section of the davit where the winch is located, which is unnecessary during winch operation

controls.name: First aid response and emergency medical services activation
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity and facilitate rapid medical treatment
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to mitigate consequences after incident occurs

supporting_text: The PIC called for assistance via handheld radio and began administering first aid to the IP
At 0756 hours, Genesis Health, Safety, Security, and Environment (HSSE) contacted a medivac service company out of Lake Charles for a medivac

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
